humanities_role_explanation: Uses factual questions from history, art, music, and pop culture as highly complex, multi-constraint QA items to test web-browsing agent capabilities.
benchmark_modalities_explanation: Text-only benchmark consisting of natural language questions requiring short-text answers verified against a ground-truth reference string.
analytical_tasks_explanation: Requires agents to satisfy complex, multi-layered constraints (e.g., identifying obscure historical matches, specific TV episodes, or academic publications matching precise authorship criteria) via strategic, multi-step web retrieval and reasoning.

general_purpose_benchmark.note: Evaluates general-purpose web browsing agents across a diverse range of topics, including science, sports, and humanities.

evaluates_generative_llm.note: Evaluates GPT-4o, GPT-4.5, OpenAI o1, and OpenAI Deep Research.

llm_is_primary_system.note: The primary systems benchmarked are LLM-based reasoning and web-browsing agents.

arts_humanities_data.note: A significant portion of the dataset covers History (9.9%), Art (10.0%), Music (9.2%), and TV shows & movies (16.2%), mapping to ASJC areas like History, Visual Arts and Performing Arts, and Music.

quantitative_metrics.note: Uses accuracy (exact equivalence evaluated by an LLM grader) and Calibration Error.

published_after_march_2023.note: Published as a preprint in April 2025.

english_language.note: The paper and all benchmark questions are in English.

summary: BrowseComp is a challenging benchmark of 1,266 questions designed to evaluate the capability of AI agents to persistently browse the web. The questions require satisfying multiple obscure constraints to find a single, easily verifiable answer, posing a significant challenge for human searchers and current LLMs. Evaluating state-of-the-art models reveals that agentic reasoning and search capabilities, like those in OpenAI Deep Research, are essential for success on these tasks.
key_findings: Standard LLMs (GPT-4o, GPT-4.5) score near 0%, and enabling basic browsing on GPT-4o only yields 1.9% accuracy. OpenAI o1 achieves 9.9% accuracy through internal reasoning. OpenAI Deep Research achieves 51.5% accuracy, which scales to over 75% when utilizing test-time compute scaling via parallel sampling and best-of-N voting.
